2026-04-21T06:34:48.9748232Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-21T06:34:48.9749564Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-21T06:34:48.9750007Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-21T06:34:48.9750377Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-21T06:34:48.9750689Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T06:34:48.9751134Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T06:34:48.9751293Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-21T06:34:48.9751469Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-21T06:34:48.9751637Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-21T06:34:48.9751862Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-21T06:34:48.9752064Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-21T06:34:48.9752346Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-21T06:34:48.9752599Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-21T06:37:46.3486255Z ◆ Now I have a good picture. The push is a migration of Azure.ResourceManager.HDInsight from AutoRest to TypeSpec (#57044). Let me analyze the README gaps:
2026-04-21T06:37:46.3486891Z   
2026-04-21T06:37:46.3487278Z   1. The README is generic - it's a boilerplate management library README with no service-specific content
2026-04-21T06:37:46.3487802Z   2. There are no samples in `tests/Samples/` directory

2026-04-21T06:37:46.4891043Z [2026-04-21T06:34:51Z] [DEBUG] [rpc] safeoutputs←resp 6468b {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"tools":[{"description":"Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{},"type":"object"},"name":"dispatch_triage"}]}}

2026-04-21T06:37:46.5569520Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-21T06:37:30.200561195Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.HDInsight`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/hdinsight/Azure.ResourceManager.HDInsight/`\n**Triggered by:** commit 9939c2e97 (PR #57044) by @live1206\n\n## What Changed\n\nThe package was migrated from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec-based code generation (#57044). New `ModelFactory` methods were added for several types (`HDInsightClusterCreateOrUpdateContent`, `HDInsightClusterRole`, `HDInsightSecurityProfile`, etc.) and API version was upgraded to include Entra User support as cluster administrator credentials.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **No service-specific Key Concepts section** — the current README links to generic SDK docs instead of describing HDInsight-specific resource types (e.g., `HDInsightClusterResource`, `HDInsightApplicationResource`, `HDInsightClusterCollection`)\n- **No code examples** — the Examples section only points to a generic samples link; there are no inline code snippets showing how to create, list, update, or delete HDInsight clusters or applications\n- **No test-backed snippets** — there is no `tests/Samples/` directory at all; the test directory only contains `Scenario/` tests\n- **Missing Entra User / new API feature documentation** — the CHANGELOG describes Entra User as cluster administrator (added in 1.2.0-beta.5) but the README does not mention this capability\n- **\"Getting started\" section is incomplete** — it lacks a concrete example of authenticating and creating a `HDInsightClusterResource` client\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n**Edit** `sdk/hdinsight/Azure.ResourceManager.HDInsight/README.md`:\n\nReplace the generic boilerplate **Key concepts** section with HDInsight-specific content:\n\n```markdown\n## Key concepts\n\n### HDInsightClusterResource\n\nAn `HDInsightClusterResource` represents a single HDInsight cluster. It exposes operations to get, update, delete, resize, rotate disk encryption keys, and retrieve monitoring/gateway settings.\n\n### HDInsightClusterCollection\n\n`HDInsightClusterCollection` is obtained from a resource group and provides operations to create, list, and check existence of HDInsight clusters.\n\n### HDInsightApplicationResource\n\n`HDInsightApplicationResource` represents an application installed on an HDInsight cluster. It exposes CRUD operations for cluster applications.\n\n### Thread safety\n\nWe guarantee that all client instance methods are thread-safe and independent of each other ([guideline](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html#dotnet-service-methods-thread-safety)).\n\n### Additional concepts\n[Client options](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#configuring-service-clients-using-clientoptions) |\n[Accessing the response](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#accessing-http-response-details-using-responset) |\n[Long-running operations](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#consuming-long-running-operations-using-operationt) |\n[Handling failures](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#reporting-errors-requestfailedexception) |\n[Diagnostics](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#logging) |\n[Mocking](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#mocking) |\n[Client lifetime](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/lifetime-management-and-thread-safety-guarantees-of-azure-sdk-net-clients/)\n```\n\nReplace the generic **Examples** section with snippet placeholders:\n\n```markdown\n## Examples\n\n### Create an HDInsight cluster\n\n```C# Snippet:HDInsightCreateCluster\n```\n```\n\n### List HDInsight clusters in a resource group\n\n```C# Snippet:HDInsightListClusters\n```\n```\n\n### Get an existing HDInsight cluster\n\n```C# Snippet:HDInsightGetCluster\n```\n```\n\n### Delete an HDInsight cluster\n\n```C# Snippet:HDInsightDeleteCluster\n```\n```\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\n**Create** `sdk/hdinsight/Azure.ResourceManager.HDInsight/tests/Samples/Sample1_Clusters.cs`:\n\n```csharp\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\n\nusing System;\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\nusing Azure.Core.TestFramework;\nusing Azure.Identity;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.HDInsight;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.HDInsight.Models;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\nusing NUnit.Framework;\n\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.HDInsight.Tests.Samples\n{\n    public class Sample1_Clusters : SamplesBase\u003cHDInsightManagementTestEnvironment\u003e\n    {\n        [Test]\n        [AsyncOnly]\n        public async Task CreateCluster()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:HDInsightCreateCluster\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n            string subscriptionId = Environment.GetEnvironmentVariable(\"AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID\");\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync()\n                .GetAwaiter().GetResult()\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(\"\u003cresource-group-name\u003e\");\n\n            HDInsightClusterCollection clusters = resourceGroup.GetHDInsightClusters();\n\n            string clusterName = \"\u003ccluster-name\u003e\";\n            HDInsightClusterCreateOrUpdateContent content = new HDInsightClusterCreateOrUpdateContent\n            {\n                Location = Azure.Core.AzureLocation.EastUS,\n                Properties = new HDInsightClusterCreateOrUpdateProperties\n                {\n                    ClusterVersion = \"5.1\",\n                    OsType = HDInsightOSType.Linux,\n                    ClusterDefinition = new HDInsightClusterDefinition\n                    {\n                        Kind = \"spark\",\n                    },\n                },\n            };\n\n            ArmOperation\u003cHDInsightClusterResource\u003e lro = await clusters.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\n                WaitUntil.Completed, clusterName, content);\n            HDInsightClusterResource cluster = lro.Value;\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Cluster '{cluster.Data.Name}' created\");\n            #endregion\n\n#if !SNIPPET\n            Assert.IsNotNull(cluster);\n            Assert.AreEqual(clusterName, cluster.Data.Name);\n#endif\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [AsyncOnly]\n        public async Task ListClusters()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:HDInsightListClusters\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync()\n                .GetAwaiter().GetResult()\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(\"\u003cresource-group-name\u003e\");\n\n            await foreach (HDInsightClusterResource cluster in resourceGroup.GetHDInsightClusters())\n            {\n                Console.WriteLine(cluster.Data.Name);\n            }\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [AsyncOnly]\n        public async Task GetCluster()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:HDInsightGetCluster\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync()\n                .GetAwaiter().GetResult()\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(\"\u003cresource-group-name\u003e\");\n\n            HDInsightClusterResource cluster = await resourceGroup.GetHDInsightClusters()\n                .GetAsync(\"\u003ccluster-name\u003e\");\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Cluster state: {cluster.Data.Properties?.ClusterState}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [AsyncOnly]\n        public async Task DeleteCluster()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:HDInsightDeleteCluster\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync()\n                .GetAwaiter().GetResult()\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(\"\u003cresource-group-name\u003e\");\n\n            HDInsightClusterResource cluster = await resourceGroup.GetHDInsightClusters()\n                .GetAsync(\"\u003ccluster-name\u003e\");\n            await cluster.DeleteAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\n            Console.WriteLine(\"Cluster deleted\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\nFor each snippet, the README placeholder (```` ```C# Snippet:\u003cName\u003e ```` followed by closing ```` ``` ````) must match the `#region Snippet:\u003cName\u003e` tag exactly.\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README at `sdk/hdinsight/Azure.ResourceManager.HDInsight/README.md` currently has all required top-level sections, but the following are incomplete or missing content:\n\n| Section | Status | Action |\n|---|---|---|\n| Getting started / Install the package | ✅ Present | No change |\n| Getting started / Prerequisites | ✅ Present | No change |\n| Getting started / Authenticate the client | ⚠️ Generic link only | Add code snippet showing `ArmClient` instantiation |\n| Key concepts | ❌ Generic link only | Replace with HDInsight-specific resource type descriptions (see Step 1) |\n| Examples | ❌ Generic link only | Replace with snippet placeholders (see Step 1) |\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change |\n| Next steps | ✅ Present | No change |\n| Contributing | ✅ Present | No change |\n\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/hdinsight/Azure.ResourceManager.HDInsight/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/hdinsight/Azure.ResourceManager.HDInsight/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 hdinsight` — injects `#region Snippet:` code into README placeholder blocks; verify with `git diff`\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 hdinsight` — regenerates public API listing (no public API changes expected here, but run to confirm)\n5. `dotnet format sdk/hdinsight/Azure.ResourceManager.HDInsight/`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e 2026-04-21T06:37:46.5949823Z server:unified callBackendTool: serverID=safeoutputs, toolName=create_issue, args=map[body:## Documentation Gap
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5949828Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5949961Z **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.HDInsight`
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5950150Z **Service directory:** `sdk/hdinsight/Azure.ResourceManager.HDInsight/`
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5950285Z **Triggered by:** commit 9939c2e97 (PR #57044) by @live1206
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5950289Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5950371Z ## What Changed
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5950375Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5951490Z The package was migrated from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec-based code generation (#57044). New `ModelFactory` methods were added for several types (`HDInsightClusterCreateOrUpdateContent`, `HDInsightClusterRole`, `HDInsightSecurityProfile`, etc.) and API version was upgraded to include Entra User support as cluster administrator credentials.
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5951638Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5951717Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5951721Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5952687Z - **No service-specific Key Concepts section** — the current README links to generic SDK docs instead of describing HDInsight-specific resource types (e.g., `HDInsightClusterResource`, `HDInsightApplicationResource`, `HDInsightClusterCollection`)
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5953388Z - **No code examples** — the Examples section only points to a generic samples link; there are no inline code snippets showing how to create, list, update, or delete HDInsight clusters or applications
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5953825Z - **No test-backed snippets** — there is no `tests/Samples/` directory at all; the test directory only contains `Scenario/` tests
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5954489Z - **Missing Entra User / new API feature documentation** — the CHANGELOG describes Entra User as cluster administrator (added in 1.2.0-beta.5) but the README does not mention this capability
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5954999Z - **"Getting started" section is incomplete** — it lacks a concrete example of authenticating and creating a `HDInsightClusterResource` client
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5955004Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5955076Z <details>
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5955285Z <summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5955297Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5955611Z This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5955616Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5955703Z ### Step 1: Modify files
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5955707Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5955883Z **Edit** `sdk/hdinsight/Azure.ResourceManager.HDInsight/README.md`:
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5955887Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5956134Z Replace the generic boilerplate **Key concepts** section with HDInsight-specific content:
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5956138Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5956207Z ```markdown
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5956285Z ## Key concepts
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5956290Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5956387Z ### HDInsightClusterResource
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5956391Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5956982Z An `HDInsightClusterResource` represents a single HDInsight cluster. It exposes operations to get, update, delete, resize, rotate disk encryption keys, and retrieve monitoring/gateway settings.
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5956986Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5957198Z ### HDInsightClusterCollection
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5957203Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5957646Z `HDInsightClusterCollection` is obtained from a resource group and provides operations to create, list, and check existence of HDInsight clusters.
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5957651Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5957747Z ### HDInsightApplicationResource
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5957750Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5958198Z `HDInsightApplicationResource` represents an application installed on an HDInsight cluster. It exposes CRUD operations for cluster applications.
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5958202Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5958279Z ### Thread safety
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5958283Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5959100Z We guarantee that all client instance methods are thread-safe and independent of each other ([guideline](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html#dotnet-service-methods-thread-safety)).
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5959106Z 
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5959188Z ### Additional concepts
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5959680Z [Client options](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#configuring-service-clients-using-clientoptions) |
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5960179Z [Accessing the response](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#accessing-http-response-details-using-responset) |
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5960701Z [Long-running operations](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#consuming-long-running-operations-using-operationt) |
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5961144Z [Handling failures](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#reporting-errors-requestfailedexception) |
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5961587Z [Diagnostics](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#logging) |
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5961877Z [Mocking](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#mocking) |
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5962346Z [Client lifetime](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/lifetime-management-and-thread-safety-guarantees-of-azure-sdk-net-clients/)
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5962415Z ```
2026-04-21T06:37:46.5962419Z
